content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic ("This app needs camera access.") and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder with no visible location feature in code (App/Info.plist:8,10).
Pierre: Apple rejects apps whose purpose strings don't explain the specific user-facing reason for a permission, and "TODO" is an instant 5.1.1 rejection. The camera string should say something like "SnapScan uses the camera to scan documents" (matching ScannerView.swift:12), and the location key should either get a real justification tied to a feature or be removed entirely since nothing in the code requests location.
